entry.title: NY Times: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Jan. 6 Panel Puts Focus on Cabinet Discussions About Removing Trump
  • Donald Trump Hits Back at Ivanka Trump’s Account That She Accepted His Election Loss
  • Biden Races to Expand Coalition Against Russia but Meets Resistance
  • McDonald’s Is Reinvented in Russia as the Economy Stumbles On
  • Should Biden Run in 2024? Democratic Whispers of ‘No’ Start to Rise.
  • Ocasio-Cortez Turns a New York Brawl Into a National Democratic Proxy Battle
  • DeSantis Event at Chelsea Piers Faces Backlash Over L.G.B.T.Q. Rights
  • Why Students Are Choosing H.B.C.U.s: ‘4 Years Being Seen as Family’
  • The Dream of the Open Road Collides With the Reality of $5-a-Gallon Gas
  • Stuckey’s Pins Revival on the Return of the Great American Road Trip
  • Gas Prices in U.S. Hit $5 a Gallon
  • Moderna’s Covid Vaccine Is Effective in Children Under 6, F.D.A. Says
  • ‘We Buried Him and Kept Walking’: Children Die as Somalis Flee Hunger
  • What the Jan. 6 Hearings Are Teaching Americans About Democracy
  • Trump, American Monster
  • Jan. 6 Committee Hearing: Heroes and Villains
  • A Single Image of Gun Violence Could Help Change Minds
  • Lockdowns Protected Older People. But at What Cost to the Health of Young Adults?
  • What Does It Mean When Greg Abbott Calls the Uvalde Gunman ‘Evil’?
  • Our Racial Reckoning Could Have Come Sooner. What Made 2020 Different?
  • The Phone Booth of the Mind
  • Feeding a Hotter Planet
  • DNA Can Identify Unknown Soldiers. It Also Reveals Old Mistakes.
  • Inflation in the United States: What You Need to Know
  • Why We Still Haven’t Solved the Unpaid Internship Problem
  • Rumbling Through Modern Jordan, a Railway From the Past
  • Newly United, French Left Hopes to Counter President in Upcoming Vote
  • Recording India’s Linguistic Riches as Leaders Push Hindi as Nation’s Tongue
  • Shortage of Artillery Ammunition Saps Ukrainian Frontline Morale
  • Jeanine Añez, Former Bolivian President, Sentenced to 10 Years in Prison
  • For South Africa’s Leader, Being a Burglary Victim Spells Trouble
  • Ukraine Live Updates: Russia Wields Greater Firepower in East, but Signs Emerge of Discord in South
  • U.S. Warns China on Taiwan as Defense Officials Meet to Cool Tensions
  • U.K. Officials Won’t Investigate Political Donation Flagged for Russian Origins
  • Single Cigarettes in Canada Will Be Inscribed With Warning
  • Pope Francis Postpones Trip to Africa, Citing Knee Problems
  • Reforms Are Imposed on U.N. Agency That Made Questionable Investments
  • A Caravan of Migrants Is Heading Toward the U.S. Border
  • Traffic-Clogged North England Bitter Over Stalled Rail Plan
  • ‘A Mass Invasion of Privacy’ but No Penalties for Tim Hortons
  • Here are the latest developments in the war in Ukraine.
  • C.D.C. Dismisses Airborne Transmission of Monkeypox. Some Experts Disagree.
  • Behind Enemy Lines, Scouts Help Ukrainians Execute Deadly Strikes
  • Royal Shipwreck From 1682 Is Found Off the Coast of England
  • Trapped by Russian Forces, Ukrainians Waited a Month in a Basement
  • When Cockatoos Become Cocka-Too Much
  • Stonewall Jackson’s Name Fell Fast, and a Fury Quickly Followed
  • Tom Rice, G.O.P. Congressman Who Voted to Impeach Trump, Stands by His Vote
  • Idaho City to Pay $11.7 Million to Man Wrongfully Convicted in 1996 Killing
  • Iraqi at Guantánamo Bay to Plead Guilty in Afghan War Crimes Case
  • How Journalists Wrestle With Covering Threats to Democracy
  • Nancy Clark Reynolds, a Player in Reagan’s Washington, Dies at 94
  • In Alaska, 48 House candidates and a first-of-its-kind special election
  • An ‘Ugly’ Inflation Report Upended Hopes That Price Gains Would Ease
  • More Than 19 Million Watched Jan. 6 Hearing, Early Ratings Show
  • Stock Markets Recoil as Investors React to Inflation Increase
  • Inflation Sped Up Again in May, Dashing Hopes for Relief
  • A Chinese Entrepreneur Who Says What Others Only Think
  • Howard Schultz: Starbucks Is Battling for the ‘Hearts and Minds’ of Workers
  • It Works for Sneakers. Now It’s for Baby PJs and Skateboards, Too.
  • James Goldston, Former TV News Chief, Helps the Jan. 6 Committee
  • After a Political Storm, Gay Days Return to Disney
  • U.S. Scrutinizes Swiss Currency Practices
  • Rents Are Climbing, Keeping Inflation High
  • Sean Thackrey, Creator of Eccentric California Wines, Dies at 79
  • Seeking a Return to Offices, Bosses Lost Leverage
  • Is It Finally Twilight for the Theater’s Sacred Monsters?
News from the New York Times

entry.title: Washington Post: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Russia controls most of Severodonetsk, governor says
  • Haiti’s gangs use TikTok, Instagram, Twitter to recruit and terrorize
  • Amid Ukraine war, husband and wife doctor team runs front-line hospital
  • Russia seeks to militarize schoolchildren and censor textbooks amid war
  • Pentagon chief says U.S. won’t ask others to choose between it and China
  • Bolivia sentences ex-president to 10 years in prison after coup trial
  • Ukraine pleas for ammunition grow amid intense firefight in East
  • Ukraine is running out of ammunition as prospects dim on the battlefield
  • Malaysia to get rid of ‘mandatory’ death sentences
  • At first meeting, U.S., China defense chiefs call for more communication
  • War, guilt and last kisses: A deceptive, uneasy calm in Kyiv
  • Venezuelan leader, Iranian president sign 20-year agreement
  • Germany to introduce some border checks during G7 summit
  • Survivor of abusive facility searches for lost Korean roots

  • Police tortured suspect in Amazon disappearance, family says

  • Canada to require a warning be printed on every cigarette.
  • Mexico City ban on bullfighting extended indefintely
  • Prosecutors want life for surviving Paris attacks member

  • Man arrested for leaving flowers on fiancee’s grave found guilty of littering

  • March for Our Lives 2022: Shooting survivors to protest gun violence
  • Ginni Thomas pressed 29 Ariz. lawmakers to help overturn Trump’s defeat, emails show

  • Post Politics Now: After dramatic first night, Jan. 6 panel plans several more high-profile hearings
  • Ivanka Trump’s Jan. 6 testimony exposes family strain
  • Biden grapples with inflation and immigration
  • Nearly 19 million watched the first Jan. 6 hearing in prime time
  • The Jan. 6 hearing was horrifying. It also gave me hope.
  • Pump shock: Why gas prices are so high
  • The Google engineer who thinks the company’s AI has come to life
  • Silicon Valley braces for tech pullback after a decade of decadence
  • Elon Musk’s Futurist Bookshelf Needs Alvin Toffler
  • What Watching ‘All the President’s Men’ Won’t Tell You
  • Have Public Health Officials Just Given Up on Covid-19?
  • Boris Johnson’s Thatcherite Housing Ideas Won’t Go Very Far
  • I’m a Texas teacher. Here are all the things I’m asked to be.
  • In Russia, a Christian religion is punished, over and over again
  • The Post endorses Brooke Lierman in Democratic primary for Maryland comptroller
  • Supreme Court justices should be able to feel safe at home
  • Taylor Lorenz said an editor was to blame. Is that okay?
  • Trump has secured his place in history
  • Cartoons by Nick Anderson, Matt Davies and Mike Luckovich
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war: ‘intense street fighting’ in Sievierodonetsk; family of Briton sentenced to death speak out – live
  • England v New Zealand: second Test, day two – live!
  • Family of UK man sentenced to death by Russia call for cooperation
  • March for Our Lives: thousands expected at US gun control protests
  • New York fund to publicly apologize for its role in Tuskegee syphilis study
  • Teenage clicks – a century of British youth culture caught on camera
  • Earwig review – more serious weirdness from Lucile Hadžihalilović
  • Rocking the Scandi style
  • On my radar: Emily Maitlis’s cultural highlights
  • Freddie Mercury’s ‘priceless’ stamp collection to be celebrated
  • Found: the ‘how to draw’ books Pablo Picasso created for his daughter
  • Liverpool agree €100m deal to sign Darwin Núñez from Benfica
  • Blacklist Dubai over failure to crack down on Russian oligarchs, say campaigners
  • Billie Eilish review – louder than ever
  • Ivanka turns on daddy but she’s just a Trump looking out for No 1 as usual | Arwa Mahdawi
  • Pete Doherty on Kate Moss: ‘Our relationship became a running battle: highs and then crushing, violent lows’
  • Pub co-owned by England cricketer Stuart Broad severely damaged in fire
  • Real Madrid wrap up €100m Aurélien Tchouaméni signing on six-year deal
  • Forget the thrill of the chase, only rarely do football’s biggest signings work out | Jonathan Wilson
  • ‘I’m not afraid of clowns’: Republican defends vote to impeach Trump
  • Zelenskiy ‘didn’t want to hear’ warnings of Russia invasion, says Biden
  • What links Eleanor Roosevelt and The Simpsons to James Callaghan’s daughter?
  • ‘I had singular focus’: 30 years on from Severn Cullis-Suzuki’s Earth Summit speech
  • The cult of Bluey: how a kids’ cartoon became a bible for modern parenting
  • Thirty years of climate summits: where have they got us?
  • DJ Fat Tony and his mother look back: ‘Even during my crack addiction, we never lost touch’
  • New York has a chance to generate all its electricity from clean energy by 2030 | Ross Barkan
  • Paapa Essiedu on grief, doubt and fury at Boris Johnson: ‘Bigotry is the backbone of his character’
  • Hidden Portugal: where to stay and what to do off the beaten track
  • 50 best summer dresses to buy, rent or thrift
  • A living room on a skateboard: how electric vehicles are redefining the car
  • ‘Forever chemicals’ are killing whales – and harming us | Ella Al-Shamahi
  • From crab pasta to griddled courgettes: Yotam Ottolenghi’s take on Italian classics – recipes
  • Shazia Mirza: ‘When I was eight, my mother said, “You are ugly, and ugly girls can’t become actresses”’
  • ‘I’m 80 and I’ve got more energy than most people’: the Arizona cheerleader squad for retirees – in pictures
  • Animal magic: why intelligence isn’t just for humans
  • Climate crisis is ‘battering our economy’ and driving inflation, new book says
  • Chinese police arrest nine after women attacked at restaurant
  • In their naked self-interest, LIV golfers are being refreshingly true to the sport’s roots | Barney Ronay
  • There’s a new breed of young, violent, far-right activist in Britain: ‘white jihadists’ | Matthew Collins
  • Are the best days now behind Belgium’s golden generation? | Jonathan Liew
  • Aukus pact: Australia pays $830m penalty for ditching non-nuclear French submarines
  • Tiepolo Blue by James Cahill review – a bold debut of psychosexual awakening
  • ET the Extra-Terrestrial at 40: Spielberg’s sci-fi smash remains a wonder
  • Pro-choice militants are targeting ‘pregnancy crisis centers’ across US
  • Anthony Albanese sees ‘no impediment’ to permanent residency for Biloela family
  • ‘The return of banditry’: Russian car industry buckles under sanctions
  • China will ‘start a war’ if Taiwan declares independence, says defence minister
  • Ginni Thomas pressed 29 lawmakers in bid to overturn Trump loss, emails show
  • Curry pours in 43 as Warriors edge Celtics to level NBA finals at two-all
  • Bolivian ex-president Jeanine Áñez jailed as leader of ‘coup’
  • Written warning on every cigarette in Canadian world-first
  • Rudy Giuliani charged with ethical misconduct over Trump’s big lie
  • Hopes fade of finding missing men as Brazilian police report finding ‘apparently human’ material
  • Justin Bieber cancels shows after half of face left paralysed by virus
  • Prince Charles criticises ‘appalling’ Rwanda migrant scheme – reports
  • Father and son killed in latest tragedy as Isle of Man TT death toll rises to five
  • Punishing heatwave expected across the US south-west this weekend
  • The ‘trial’ in Donetsk has set a chilling precedent for conflict in the 21st century | Sadakat Kadri
  • Democrats promise more on Trump’s January 6 role after dramatic TV hearing
Latest international news, sport and comment from the Guardian

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 11 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-11 14:10PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong’s chief justice stresses importance of senior counsel, as 5 barristers appointed to prestigious rank
  • Hong Kong has proposed banning CBD products within a year. Here’s everything you need to know
  • Hong Kong homebuyers snap up Sun Hung Kai’s Silicon Hill flats for the second weekend in a row, aiming to get ahead of higher interest rates
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong’s status at risk if it doesn’t fully open up to international travel, leader warns, as 851 new Covid-19 cases logged
  • Hong Kong protests: ‘no regrets’ over extradition bill bid, Carrie Lam insists, saying apology in 2019 was for poor communication on legislation
  • Largest international school group in Hong Kong to launch trial run for pre-nursery classes, despite city’s declining student population
  • Three steps for Hong Kong to reconnect with the world and regain its international lustre
  • More than 540,000 Hongkongers issued BN(O) passports since 2019 with most approved after UK citizenship scheme unveiled
  • Hong Kong unveils strategy for containing possible monkeypox outbreak but shies away from travel bans
  • Native-speaking English teachers in Hong Kong government schools told to declare allegiance to city or face dismissal
  • Chinese defence officials take aim at US’ ‘aggressive’ Taiwan-Ukraine comparison
  • Covid cases bounce back in Taiwan after week of declines
  • ‘Asian water tower’ is facing a worsening supply imbalance, study finds
  • Man in China who stole US$3,700 from a classmate on his wedding day, then ‘loaned’ back some stolen cash to groom gets 2 years jail
  • Nine held in Chinese city after fierce attack on women diners sends two to hospital
  • Can vaccination or drugs help defeat long Covid?
  • Canada says it’s raised issue of China’s recent ‘concerning’ aerial behaviour through diplomatic channels
  • China’s new Covid-19 normal means ‘endless testing’, bewildering system, anxiety over expired results
  • Chinese-Canadian rapper Kris Wu tried for rape by Beijing court
  • Coronavirus: China’s jobless rate could reach 2020 levels unless Beijing ‘learns’ early pandemic lessons, experts say
  • China’s hostile environment for Western tech pushed out Amazon and Airbnb, but competition remains the biggest challenge
  • US defence chief Lloyd Austin says China is getting ‘more coercive’ in its territorial claims
  • Covid-19 data points to animal origin but more studies needed to spot what and where: WHO advisers
  • Senior China brass rebuts Kishida’s claim Beijing, not Tokyo, disrupted East China Sea status quo
  • Ukraine war casts shadow over China’s belt and road ties with Russia-led Eurasian trade bloc
  • Asean should have a ‘full-time’ Myanmar special envoy with added powers, Malaysia’s Saifuddin Abdullah says
  • China-Australia relations: Canberra says it values China trade ties, urges Beijing to be ‘transparent’ about military build-up
  • India police shoot 2 during protests over BJP official’s Prophet Mohammed remarks
  • Indian ‘granfluencers’ take Instagram by storm with sex education, knitting lessons
  • Foxconn’s Vietnam workers poached as hiring battle heats up amid US-China trade tensions
  • Australia reaches US$584m settlement with France’s Naval Group for axing submarine deal
  • Marcos touts ties with ‘good friend’ Beijing as Philippines issues new South China Sea protest
  • North Korea appoints first woman foreign minister as Kim Jong-un doubles down on arms buildup
  • China’s ‘walking on two legs’ conundrum: how to balance zero-Covid and the economy?
  • As Chinese buyer drops US$61m on 20 condo units, what’s fuelling Singapore’s home prices?
  • We live in the age of nasty metaverse wars. Is there no escape from this hell?
  • World’s poorest nations suffering the fallout of a heating planet need cash now, UN climate talks
  • Diplomacy, punitive sanctions only useful as ‘pre-emptive’ measures to stop wars, Zelensky says at Shangri-La Dialogue
  • Joe Biden slams Big Oil over profits: ‘Exxon made more money than God this year’
  • Rare Stradivarius used in Wizard of Oz soundtrack sells for near-record US$15.3 million in New York auction
  • Joe Biden says Ukraine’s Zelensky brushed off warnings of Russia’s invasion
  • Brazil police find apparent human remains in river where UK reporter vanished
  • Justin Bieber opens up on half-paralysed face: ‘I have Ramsay Hunt Syndrome’
  • UK’s Boris Johnson had a bad week. Here are the next potential flashpoints
  • Ukraine pleads for more weapons, as cholera spreads
  • US dropping Covid-19 tests for incoming air travel
  • Ukraine: Chronic hunger for 19 million more over next year due to war, UN food agency warns
  • New Oriental’s Yu Minhong brings back English teaching with a live-streaming e-commerce twist
  • Coronavirus: China adds 138 new cases on Friday, as Beijing and Shanghai conduct more mass testing
  • Tesla proposes 3-to-1 stock split as Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, friend of Elon Musk, prepares to leave board
  • Coronavirus lockdowns cost China’s car sector another 400,000 vehicles in May, though ‘revenge spending’ may soon provide a boost
  • Global hunt for Russian billionaires’ superyachts won’t end Ukraine war
  • Will India benefit as China’s Covid-19 lockdowns hit Apple supply chains?
  • The right swimsuit for you? It’s whatever you like regardless of your body type – low-cut or high-leg, bikini or one-piece
  • Ms Marvel directors hope Marvel’s first Muslim superhero series inspires ‘a lot of girls in the world’ as well as people of any religion, race or genders
  • Amid China’s civil war between Communists and Nationalists, how two sisters were separated by fate and kept apart through their successes
  • Breathwork, movement, sound and social interaction create ‘core steadiness’ you need to relieve stress and anxiety in the face of change, yoga therapist says
  • All of Us Are Dead, Hellbound and Taxi Driver set for season 2; Jang Na-ra, Jang Hyuk reunite for Family – K-drama casting latest
  • Up close with elephants at sanctuary in Thailand, volunteers shovelling dung and sorting mangoes gain insights on the challenges of conservation amid Covid-19 crisis
  • How global warming sparked pair’s sustainable architecture with its focus on natural ventilation and high-rise living connected to nature
  • ‘Historical nihilists’ know that the truth ultimately seeps through, eroding that which attempts to conceal it
  • Singapore’s Gillman Barracks arts centre to become lifestyle destination after several major galleries give up their leases
  • Meet Princess Märtha Louise of Norway: newly engaged to Gwyneth Paltrow’s spirit guide Durek Verrett, the 50-year-old royal claims to talk to horses and once opened an ‘angel school’
  • Inside Melania Trump’s US$100,000 shoe collection: from Valentino and Christian Louboutin, to Jimmy Choo and Manolo Blahnik, she’s got 130 pairs in New York alone …
  • Who is Britney’s low-key brother Bryan Spears? The ‘only’ family member invited to her secret wedding to Sam Asghari once said his sister’s conservatorship was ‘a great thing’ ...
  • Who’s the richest Knowles-Carter? The family’s net worths, ranked: from Jay-Z and Beyoncé’s billion-dollar empire, to Solange’s solo success and Kelly Rowland and Michelle Williams’ Destiny’s Child millions
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Asakusa retailers get ready to roll out the red carpet to tourists
  • Penal provisions to focus on rehabilitation not punishment
  • Japan's defence minister slams nuclear neighbours who 'ignore rules'
  • Diet gender gap needs fixing, say bulk of Lower House members
  • Kishida blasts changing status quo through use of military force
  • Shiseido shifts focus to quality products that are made in Japan
  • U.S., Chinese defence chiefs stand firm over Taiwan in first meeting
  • N.Korea's Kim vows 'power to power' military; promotes nuclear envoy
  • US: China's military activity around Taiwan threatens region
  • Murakami opens up on his latest translation of F. Scott Fitzgerald
  • VOX POPULI: Leaders deserve to be judged for trying, not just failed diplomacy
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 11 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
